pages.raw_text:
The Spokane trades council introduc-
ed in the city council last night the
resolutions prepared by the council
a week ago which declare that the
city had been infested for years by a
clique of confidence men operating
under the guise of employment agen-
cies and thriving upon the hard-earned
dollars dishonesty wrung from the
wageearners; that through the expo
sure made by the Press il is made man.
fest to all that the same fiends in
human form have descended to the
lowest depths of iniquity and become
procurers of inmates for hells of vice.


The resolutions condemned the en-
ployment agencies of Spokane as a
menace to the public morals in that
they rob the unwary and aid in the
seduction and debauohery of young
girls and women.


The trades council in the resolutions
called upon the civil authorities TO
withdraw the licenses from these dens
of vice. The city council is also urged
to establish and operate free en-
ployment agency at some central point
in the city.


The resolution is signed by w. f.
Martin, president, and T. S. Heskett,
secretary. The council referred the
resolutions to the finance committee.
